chapter_title.en: Chapter 31 – Satisfaction with the Judgement (of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/6)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/6)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: يَنْبَغِي لِمَنْ عَقَلَ عَنِ اللَّهِ أَنْ لَا يَسْتَبْطِئَهُ فِي رِزْقِهِ وَ لَا يَتَّهِمَهُ فِي قَضَائِهِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, Bin Abu Nasr, from Safwan Al Jammal,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> the 1<sup>st</sup> having said: ‘It is befitting for the one who minds about Allah<sup>azwj</sup> that he does not consider Him<sup>azwj</sup> as being slow regarding his sustenance, nor accuse him regarding His<sup>azwj</sup> Judgement’.
en.sarwar: 5. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn abu Nasr from Safwan al-Jammal from abu al-Hassan, the 1 st ’ recipient of divine supreme covenant, who has said the following: “One who has proper understanding about Allah must not feel that Allah procrastinates in sending his sustenance or accuse Him in His decisions.”
